---
title: "Integrating CADD & BioPython For Biomedical Research"
categories: [Courses]
image: "image.jpg"
---
<div style="text-align: justify;">
**Course Overview:**  
This course focuses on integrating Computational Drug Design (CADD) and Python programming (BioPython) to address biomedical research problems. Students will learn how to analyze protein structures, study protein-ligand interactions, and apply computational tools for drug discovery and biomedical data analysis.  

**What Students Will Learn:**  
- Basics of Computational Drug Design (CADD) and molecular docking  
- Protein structure analysis and visualization  
- Practical Python programming using BioPython for biological data  
- Workflow integration of CADD and BioPython for biomedical research projects  
- Data analysis, interpretation, and reporting in a research context  

**Who Should Attend:**  
- Students in bioinformatics, biotechnology, and life sciences  
- Beginners in computational drug design and Python programming  
- Anyone interested in applying computational approaches to biomedical research  

**Course Outcomes:**  
By the end of this course, students will be able to:  
- Apply CADD techniques to study protein-ligand interactions  
- Use BioPython to manipulate and analyze biological datasets  
- Integrate computational tools to support biomedical research projects  
- Develop skills for research-focused problem solving in bioinformatics and drug discovery

</div>
